notes:
Expedition Base Set (ecard1), first e-Card-series set, English release Sept 2002. CONFIRMED pack structure (Bulbapedia): packs reduced from 11 to 9 cards = 5 Common + 2 Uncommon + 1 Reverse Holofoil ("refractor" reverse, new to e-Card) + 1 Rare-or-Rare-Holo. Booster box = 36 packs (ToyWiz "36 Packs", DA Card World). No ETB (did not exist in this era).

RARE SLOT: holo ~1 in 3 packs (era standard, corroborated by community per-holo ~1.03% giving ~12 holos/box across 36 packs; elitefourum confirms a holo does NOT replace the non-holo rare — every pack still yields a non-holo rare and may additionally yield a holo). Modeled rare slot as rare-holo 1/3, rare 2/3. The chase "rare-holo" foil hit bucket is produced only by this slot.

REVERSE SLOT: e-Card refractor reverse, distributed RARITY-FLAT across the first 159 singles (elitefourum: "every single card individually has the same odds in the reverse spot"; per-card ~0.63%). A reverse-holo of a rare is the reverse printing of the rare (maps to the `rare` bucket), NOT the chase foil `rare-holo` bucket. Per the WOTC/e-Card era directive, modeled the added reverse-holo slot as 50/50 common/uncommon filler so as not to inflate the hit rate; the small share of reverse rares is folded out for simplicity and does not affect the chase rare-holo EV.

Confidence MEDIUM: pack composition and box size are firmly sourced (Bulbapedia + retail listings); the 1-in-3 holo rate is an era-average corroborated by community samples rather than a large per-set study.

Hit bucket for EV = rare-holo (the foil chase cards). Vintage sealed box carries a large collector premium, so EV/booster is expected to sit well below box price — rates were NOT inflated to compensate.

Bucket check: distribution keys are a subset of available buckets {rare-holo, rare, uncommon, common}. Each slot sums to exactly 1.0. Slot count = 5 common + 2 uncommon + 1 reverse + 1 rare = 9 = packSize.
